name: Shiga Prefecture (Japan)

colors.symbolism: the waters of Lake Biwa and the bright future of Shiga
purity, harmony and the development of the prefecture

description: A blue field charged at the centre with a white emblem combining the katakana シ and ガ of the prefecture's name into a wave-like form.
history: Shiga's prefectural symbol was adopted on 11 May 1957. The flag, with the white emblem on a blue field, has been in use as the prefectural flag since the same year.

features.symbolism: the waters of Lake Biwa, the largest lake in Japan, around which the prefecture is centred

features.role: Shiga prefectural emblem (stylised katakana シ and ガ)
features.symbolism: a stylised combination of the katakana シ (shi) and ガ (ga) forming the prefecture's name (シガ, Shiga), arranged to suggest waves of Lake Biwa rising in harmony.
